skill.name: dependency-updater
skill.description: Smart dependency management for multi-language projects. It detects package files, applies safe dependency updates, prompts for major versions, and runs ecosystem audit tools.
skill.summary: Update and audit project dependencies across common language ecosystems.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns. Review found the weak-crypto and URL findings are documentation false positives, but the skill intentionally runs package manager and audit commands that can modify project files and fetch packages. No malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Dependency Tool Commands Modify Project State
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run package manager commands such as taze, pip-review, go get, cargo update, bundle update, Maven, and dotnet tooling. This is expected for a dependency updater, but it can change manifests, lock files, and installed dependency trees.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented as the main workflow and are intended to update dependencies. The risk is real operational impact, not evidence of malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Security Fix and Install Commands May Execute Ecosystem Behavior
medium_findings.description: The workflow recommends install and audit-fix commands after updates. Package manager installs and fixes can reach registries, run lifecycle scripts, or alter lock files depending on the ecosystem and project configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced commands are standard dependency operations with known side effects. The skill frames them as legitimate maintenance steps and does not hide their purpose.

low_findings.title: Shell Wrapper Invokes Requested Tool
low_findings.description: The check-tool helper accepts a tool name and runs its version command. This is a limited diagnostic helper, but it still executes a command chosen by the caller.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wrapper is simple and transparent, and a local caller could already run shell commands. The concern is limited to expected local command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations are front matter, prose, or dependency-resolution examples. No cryptographic hashing or encryption implementation was found at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows ordinary documentation text. There is no algorithm call, cryptographic API, or security-sensitive implementation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to public GitHub pages for related dependency tools. They are not used in executable code and do not receive local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in related-tool tables. No network request code or data transfer path was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Manager Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on external package tools to inspect, update, install, and audit dependencies. These commands should be run only in trusted repositories with user review for major updates.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command execution pattern is central to the skill and clearly documented. It is risky by capability, but consistent with the declared purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Cleanup Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The emergency repair guidance includes removal of dependency directories, lock files, virtual environments, and module sums. These commands can delete local project state if used without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The listed commands are explicit cleanup examples. They are legitimate recovery actions but can cause data loss in a repository if applied casually.

content.value_statement: Dependency maintenance is slow and inconsistent across language ecosystems. This skill detects project types, applies safe updates, prompts for risky changes, and runs audit tools.
actual_capabilities: Detects common package files for Node.js, Python, Go, Rust, Ruby, Java, and .NET projects.
Classifies dependency changes as fixed, patch, minor, or major updates.
Applies minor and patch updates automatically when the package ecosystem supports it.
Prompts users before applying major version updates that may contain breaking changes.
Runs or recommends ecosystem audit tools such as npm audit, pip-audit, govulncheck, cargo audit, and bundle audit.
Provides diagnosis guidance for conflicts, peer dependencies, unused dependencies, and duplicate packages.
limitations: It depends on local package managers and update tools being installed.
It cannot guarantee that minor or patch updates are fully backward compatible.
It may modify manifests, lock files, and installed dependency directories.
It does not replace test suites, changelog review, or human approval for major upgrades.

use_cases.title: Routine Project Maintenance
use_cases.description: Update routine dependencies, refresh lock files, and review major version prompts before opening a maintenance pull request.

use_cases.title: Security Remediation
use_cases.description: Run dependency audit tools, identify vulnerable packages, and apply safer upgrade paths based on severity.

use_cases.title: Multi-Language Repository Cleanup
use_cases.description: Detect package ecosystems in a repository and choose the right update workflow for each project area.

prompt_templates.title: Check for Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this project for outdated dependencies. Show what can be updated safely and what needs my approval.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Safe Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Update minor and patch dependencies only. Do not apply major updates without asking me first.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit dependencies for vulnerabilities, summarize severity, and recommend the safest fix path.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Dependency Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose why dependency installation is failing. Inspect conflicts, peer dependencies, lock files, and propose a minimal fix.

output_examples.input: Update my dependencies in this Node.js project.
output: Detected a Node.js project with package.json.
Applied available patch and minor updates.
Found two major updates that require approval.
Recommended running the test suite before committing lock file changes.

output_examples.input: Audit my Python dependencies for security issues.
output: Suggested targeted package upgrades and verification steps.

output_examples.input: My dependency install fails after an update.
output: Identified likely version or peer dependency conflicts.
Listed cleanup steps only when simpler fixes are not enough.

best_practices: Commit or stash work before running dependency updates.
Review changelogs and migration notes before approving major versions.
Run installation, audits, and tests before merging dependency changes.
anti_patterns: Do not approve every major update without reviewing breaking changes.
Do not ignore lock file changes after updating dependencies.
Do not run cleanup commands in a repository with uncommitted work.

faq.question: Does this skill update dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: It can apply minor and patch updates automatically. It should ask before applying major versions.

faq.question: Which ecosystems does it support?
faq.answer: It covers Node.js, Python, Go, Rust, Ruby, Java, and .NET workflows described in the skill files.

faq.question: Can it fix security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can run or recommend ecosystem audit tools and suggest update paths. Some vulnerabilities require manual migration work.

faq.question: Will it change my lock files?
faq.answer: Yes. Dependency updates and installs can change lock files, manifests, and installed packages.

faq.question: Is it safe for production repositories?
faq.answer: Use it with source control, review major updates, and run tests before merging changes.

faq.question: Does it install missing tools automatically?
faq.answer: The skill suggests installation commands for missing tools. Users should approve installations before running them.
